name: Pickled Beets
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 8 pints
Procedure:
1. Trim beet tops, leaving a 1-inch stem and roots to prevent color bleeding. Wash thoroughly. Sort by size, covering similar sizes together in boiling water. Cook until tender (about 25 to 30 minutes). Drain and discard the liquid. Cool beets.
2. Remove skins and trim roots and stems. Slice into 1/4-inch slices. Peel and thinly slice onions.
3. In a large pot, combine vinegar, salt, sugar, and water. Place spices in a cheesecloth bag and add to the vinegar mixture. Bring to a boil.
4. Add beets and onions. Simmer for 5 minutes. Remove spice bag.
5. Pack hot jars with beets and onions, leaving a 1/2-inch headspace. Pour hot vinegar solution into jars, allowing a 1/2-inch headspace.
6. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1.
Variation:
Pickled whole baby beets. Follow the above directions but use beets that are 1- to 1-1/2 inches in diameter. Pack whole; do not slice. Onions may be omitted.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Pickled Beets in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints or Quarts.
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 30 minutes
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 45 minutes
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 40 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 45 minutes
